vulnerability.title: Enable Certmap in SSSD
vulnerability.description: SSSD should be configured to verify the certificate of the user or group. To set this up ensure that section like certmap/testing.test/rule_name is setup in /etc/sssd/sssd.conf. For example [certmap/testing.test/rule_name] matchrule =<SAN>.*EDIPI@mil maprule = (userCertificate;binary={cert!bin}) domains = testing.test
vulnerability.recommendation: Review and remediate: Enable Certmap in SSSD

triage_decision.reason: Remediation consists of adding a certmap stanza to /etc/sssd/sssd.conf and restarting the sssd service. This change does not affect bootloader, partitions, or critical network services. Although it modifies authentication configuration, it follows standard STIG hardening patterns and, when applied correctly, does not inherently lock out SSH or console access.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential misconfiguration could lead to authentication failures; Requires SSSD service restart
